content_gaps: The distinction between the symbolism of different bee species (honeybee, bumblebee, etc.) is rarely discussed.
Most pages mention Egyptian and Greek mythology but offer very little detail or specific stories beyond a sentence or two.
The connection between bee symbolism and specific materials in jewelry (e.g., gold for honey, amber for resin, gemstones for flowers) is not explored.
There is a lack of content on the darker or more protective aspects of bee symbolism, such as the meaning of the sting or the bee as a guardian of the hive and other worlds.
The role of bees in Celtic and Druidic traditions is often mentioned but not elaborated on in any meaningful way.
